A_intro:
& half day. Part of the class time will be devoted to field work with hoes and
machettes; one lucky boy will get to run the school tractor which has been
A_methods:
provided through the courtesy of the program of Assistance to International
Development. It may rein but the field work will continue. Classes recess at
mid-day from 11 a.m. until 2 p.m. Other subjects are studied in the school for
the remaining half day.
At times shop work is included. A shop building has been built, agein
through the courtesy of AID. However, parents and village people helped construct
the shop building and nearly everyone contributed to the building fund. Some of
the poorer people could purchase only one cement block but they did that
willingly and contributed their time and labor to help construct the building.
A_results:
The teachers of vocational agriculture have ‘been trained in other countries,
mostly the United States. Supervised farming activities are on the school farm
end some farm animals are being raised cooperatively. The local bank has backed
the informal cooperative group of students of vocational agriculture in the renting
of some land for the purpose of raising a crop of sugar cane.
Future Farmers Chapters are a new part of the program. There is no national
organization but local groups have been formed. There are no funds for national
meetings or awards programs. The main objectives of the program are to learn to
work efficiently with what is available. .
“The program of vocational agriculture grows rapidly in Costa Rica. It
promises to contribute to the agricultural development in Coste Rica what it
has to egriculture in the United States.
Dr. Agan studied the vocational agriculture program in Costa Rica for 90 days
during the past summer.
Journal of the American Association of
Teacher Educators in Agriculture
Volume 4, Number 2, pp.20
DOI: 10.5032/jaatea.1963.02020
